briefdescription.para: edges_: EdgeId -> edge data
edgePerVertex_: VertId -> one edge id of one of edges with origin there
each true bit here corresponds to valid element in edgePerVertex_
edgePerFace_: FaceId -> one edge id with this face at left
each true bit here corresponds to valid element in edgePerFace_
the number of valid elements in edgePerVertex_ or set bits in validVerts_
the number of valid elements in edgePerFace_ or set bits in validFaces_
if false, validVerts_, validFaces_, numValidVerts_, numValidFaces_ are not updated
creates an edge not associated with any vertex or face
checks whether the edge is disconnected from all other edges and disassociated from all vertices and faces (as if after makeEdge)
returns last not lone undirected edge id, or invalid id if no such edge exists
returns last not lone edge id, or invalid id if no such edge exists
remove all lone edges from given set
returns the number of half-edge records including lone ones
returns the number of allocated edge records
returns the number of undirected edges (pairs of half-edges) including lone ones
returns the number of allocated undirected edges (pairs of half-edges)
computes the number of not-lone (valid) undirected edges
finds and returns all not-lone (valid) undirected edges
sets the capacity of half-edges vector
returns true if given edge is within valid range and not-lone
returns the amount of memory this object occupies on heap
requests the removal of unused capacity

detaileddescription.para: given two half edges do either of two: 1) if a and b were from distinct rings, puts them in one ring; 2) if a and b were from the same ring, puts them in separate rings; the cut in rings in both cases is made after a and b

memberdef.argsstring: (EdgeId e, const std::function< void(EdgeId del, EdgeId rem)> &onEdgeDel)
memberdef.name: collapseEdge

param.type: const std::function< void(EdgeId del, EdgeId rem)> &

detaileddescription.para:
collapses given edge in a vertex and deletes 1) faces: left( e ) and right( e ); 2) edges: e, next( e.sym() ), prev( e.sym() ), and optionally next( e ), prev( e ) if their left and right triangles are deleted; 3) all vertices that lost their last edge; calls onEdgeDel for every deleted edge (del) including given (e); if valid (rem) is given then dest( del ) = dest( rem ) and their origins are in different ends of collapsing edge, (rem) shall take the place of (del)
prev( e ) if it is still valid

briefdescription.para: next (counter clock wise) half-edge in the origin ring
previous (clock wise) half-edge in the origin ring
returns origin vertex of half-edge
returns destination vertex of half-edge
returns left face of half-edge
returns right face of half-edge

detaileddescription.para: sets new origin to the full origin ring including this edge; edgePerVertex_ table is updated accordingly
sets new left face to the full left ring including this edge; edgePerFace_ table is updated accordingly

briefdescription.para: returns true if a and b are both from the same origin ring
returns true if a and b are both from the same left face ring
returns the number of edges around the origin vertex, returns 1 for lone edges
returns the number of edges around the given vertex
returns the number of edges around the left face: 3 for triangular faces, ...
returns the number of edges around the given face: 3 for triangular faces, ...
returns true if the cell to the left of a is triangular

detaileddescription.para: gets 3 vertices of given triangular face; the vertices are returned in counter-clockwise order if look from mesh outside

briefdescription.para: This one is not in the bindings because of the reference-to-array parameter.
return true if triangular face (f) has (v) as one of its vertices
returns three vertex ids for valid triangles, invalid triangles are skipped

detaileddescription.para: returns three vertex ids for valid triangles (which can be accessed by FaceId), vertex ids for invalid triangles are undefined, and shall not be read

gets 3 vertices of the left face ( face-id may not exist, but the shape must be triangular) the vertices are returned in counter-clockwise order if look from mesh outside: v0 = org( a ), v1 = dest( a )

briefdescription.para: This one is not in the bindings because of the reference-to-array parameter.

detaileddescription.para: if given point is 1) in vertex, then invokes callback once with it; 2) on edge, then invokes callback twice with every vertex of the edge; 3) inside triangle, then invokes callback trice with every vertex of the triangle

given one edge with triangular face on the left; returns two other edges of the same face, oriented to have this face on the left; the edges are returned in counter-clockwise order if look from mesh outside
gets 3 edges of given triangular face, oriented to have it on the left; the edges are returned in counter-clockwise order if look from mesh outside

briefdescription.para: This one is not in the bindings because of the reference-to-array parameter.

detaileddescription.para: returns true if the cell to the left of a is quadrangular

briefdescription.para: for all valid vertices this vector contains an edge with the origin there
returns valid edge if given vertex is present in the mesh
returns true if given vertex is present in the mesh
returns the number of valid vertices
returns last valid vertex id, or invalid id if no single valid vertex exists
creates new vert-id not associated with any edge yet
explicitly increases the size of vertices vector
explicitly increases the size of vertices vector, doubling the current capacity if it was not enough
sets the capacity of vertices vector
returns the number of vertex records including invalid ones
returns the number of allocated vert records
returns cached set of all valid vertices
sets in (vs) all valid vertices that were not selected before the call, and resets other bits
if region pointer is not null then converts it in reference, otherwise returns all valid vertices in the mesh

inbodydescription.para: region shall be either given on input or maintained in validVerts_
if region is given and all valid vertices are known, then region must be a subset of them

briefdescription.para: for all valid faces this vector contains an edge with that face at left
returns valid edge if given vertex is present in the mesh
returns true if given face is present in the mesh
if two valid faces share the same edge then it is found and returned
if two valid edges share the same vertex then it is found and returned as Edge with this vertex in origin
if two valid faces share the same vertex then it is found and returned as Edge with this vertex in origin
if two valid edges belong to same valid face then it is found and returned
returns the number of valid faces
returns last valid face id, or invalid id if no single valid face exists
creates new face-id not associated with any edge yet
deletes the face, also deletes its edges and vertices if they were not shared by other faces and not in
deletes multiple given faces by calling deleteFace for each
explicitly increases the size of faces vector
explicitly increases the size of faces vector, doubling the current capacity if it was not enough
sets the capacity of faces vector
returns the number of face records including invalid ones
returns the number of allocated face records
returns cached set of all valid faces
sets in (fs) all valid faces that were not selected before the call, and resets other bits
if region pointer is not null then converts it in reference, otherwise returns all valid faces in the mesh

inbodydescription.para: region shall be either given on input or maintained in validFaces_
if region is given and all valid faces are known, then region must be a subset of them

detaileddescription.para: returns the first boundary edge (for given region or for whole mesh if region is nullptr) in counter-clockwise order starting from given edge with the same left face or hole; returns invalid edge if no boundary edge is found
returns true if left(e) is a valid (region) face, and it has a boundary edge (isBdEdge(e,region) == true)
returns a boundary edge with given left face considering boundary of given region (or for whole mesh if region is nullptr); returns invalid edge if no boundary edge is found

briefdescription.para: returns true if given face belongs to the region and it has a boundary edge (isBdEdge(e,region) == true)
returns all faces for which isBdFace(f, region) is true
return true if left face of given edge belongs to region (or just have valid id if region is nullptr)
return true if given edge is inner for given region (or for whole mesh if region is nullptr)

detaileddescription.para: isBdEdge(e) returns true, if the edge (e) is a boundary edge of the mesh: (e) has a hole from one or both sides. isBdEdge(e, region) returns true, if the edge (e) is a boundary edge of the given region: (e) has a region's face from one side (region.test(f0)==true) and a hole or not-region face from the other side (!f1 || region.test(f1)==false). If the region contains all faces of the mesh then isBdEdge(e) is the union of isBdEdge(e, region) and not-lone edges without both left and right faces.

briefdescription.para: returns all (test) edges for which left(e) does not belong to the region and isBdEdge(e, region) is true

detaileddescription.para: returns the first boundary edge (for given region or for whole mesh if region is nullptr) in counter-clockwise order starting from given edge with the same origin; returns invalid edge if no boundary edge is found

briefdescription.para: returns true if edge's origin is on (region) boundary

detaileddescription.para: returns a boundary edge with given vertex in origin considering boundary of given region (or for whole mesh if region is nullptr); returns invalid edge if no boundary edge is found

briefdescription.para: returns true if given vertex is on (region) boundary
returns all (test) vertices for which isBdVertex(v, region) is true
returns true if one of incident faces of given vertex pertain to given region (or any such face exists if region is nullptr)
returns true if left face of given edge belongs to given region (if provided) and right face either does not exist or does not belong
return true if given edge is inner or boundary for given region (or for whole mesh if region is nullptr), returns false for lone edges
given a (region) boundary edge with no right face in given region, returns next boundary edge for the same region: dest(e)==org(res)
given a (region) boundary edge with no right face in given region, returns previous boundary edge for the same region; dest(res)==org(e)
finds and returns edge from o to d in the mesh; returns invalid edge otherwise
returns true if the mesh (region) does not have any neighboring holes

detaileddescription.para: returns one edge with no valid left face for every boundary in the mesh; if region is given, then returned edges must have valid right faces from the region

briefdescription.para: returns full edge-loop of left face from (e) starting from (e) itself

detaileddescription.para: returns full edge-loops of left faces from every edge in (es); each edge-loop will be returned only once even if some faces are represented by more than one edge in (es)

briefdescription.para: returns all boundary edges, where each edge does not have valid left face
returns all vertices incident to path edges
returns all valid left faces of path edges
returns all valid right faces of path edges

detaileddescription.para: given the edge with left and right triangular faces, which form together a quadrangle, rotates the edge counter-clockwise inside the quadrangle
tests all edges e having valid left and right faces and org(e0) == dest(next(e)); if the test has passed, then flips the edge so increasing the degree of org(e0)
tests all edges e having valid left and right faces and v == dest(next(e)); if the test has passed, then flips the edge so increasing the degree of vertex v
tests all edges e having valid left and right faces and org(e0) == org(e); if the test has passed, then flips the edge so decreasing the degree of org(e0)
tests all edges e having valid left and right faces and v == org(e); if the test has passed, then flips the edge so decreasing the degree of vertex v
split given edge on two parts: dest(returned-edge) = org(e) - newly created vertex, org(returned-edge) = org(e-before-split), dest(e) = dest(e-before-split)

left and right faces of given edge if valid are also subdivided on two parts each; the split edge will keep both face IDs and their degrees, and the new edge will have new face IDs and new faces are triangular; if left or right faces of the original edge were in the region, then include new parts of these faces in the region
new2Old
receive mapping from newly appeared triangle to its original triangle (part to full)
split given triangle on three triangles, introducing new vertex (which is returned) inside original triangle and connecting it to its vertices

if region is given, then it must include (f) and new faces will be added there as well
new2Old
receive mapping from newly appeared triangle to its original triangle (part to full)

flip orientation (normals) of
all mesh elements if
fullComponents
is nullptr, or
given mesh components in
fullComponents.
The behavior is undefined if fullComponents is given but there are connected components with some edges included and not-included there.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MeshTopology &from, FaceMap *outFmap=nullptr, VertMap *outVmap=nullptr, WholeEdgeMap *outEmap=nullptr, bool rearrangeTriangles=false)
memberdef.name: addPart

briefdescription.para: returns mappings: from.id -> this.id
the same but copies only portion of (from) specified by fromFaces,
This is skipped in the bindings because it conflicts with the overload taking a pointer in C#. Since that overload is strictly more useful, we're keeping that one.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MeshTopology &from, const FaceBitSet *fromFaces, bool flipOrientation=false, const std::vector< EdgePath > &thisContours={}, const std::vector< EdgePath > &fromContours={}, const PartMapping &map={})
memberdef.name: addPartByMask

detaileddescription.para:
this version has more parameters
flipOrientation
if true then every from triangle is inverted before adding
thisContours
contours on this mesh (no left face) that have to be stitched with
fromContours
contours on from mesh during addition (no left face if flipOrientation otherwise no right face)

memberdef.argsstring: (const MeshTopology &from, const FaceBitSet &fromFaces, bool flipOrientation=false, const std::vector< EdgePath > &thisContours={}, const std::vector< EdgePath > &fromContours={}, const PartMapping &map={})
memberdef.name: addPartByMask

briefdescription.para: This is skipped in the bindings because it conflicts with the overload taking a pointer in C#. Since that overload is strictly more useful, we're keeping that one.
for each triangle selects edgeWithLeft with minimal origin vertex

detaileddescription.para:
tightly packs all arrays eliminating lone edges and invalid faces and vertices
outFmap outVmap outEmap
if given returns mappings: old.id -> new.id;
rearrangeTriangles
if true then calls rotateTriangles() and selects the order of triangles according to the order of their vertices
tightly packs all arrays eliminating lone edges and invalid faces and vertices; reorder all faces, vertices and edges according to given maps, each containing old id -> new id mapping

tightly packs all arrays eliminating lone edges and invalid faces and vertices; reorder all faces, vertices and edges according to given maps, each containing old id -> new id mapping; unlike pack method, this method allocates minimal amount of memory for its operation but works much slower

briefdescription.para: saves in binary stream

detaileddescription.para:
loads from binary stream
text of error if any

briefdescription.para: compare that two topologies are exactly the same

detaileddescription.para: These function are for parallel mesh creation from different threads. If you are not sure, do not use them.
resizes all internal vectors and sets the numbers of valid elements in preparation for addPackedPart; edges are resized without initialization (so the user must initialize them using addPackedPart)

memberdef.argsstring: (const MeshTopology &from, EdgeId toEdgeId, const FaceMap &fmap, const VertMap &vmap)
memberdef.name: addPackedPart

detaileddescription.para: compute 1) numValidVerts_ and validVerts_ from edgePerVertex_ 2) numValidFaces_ and validFaces_ from edgePerFace_ and activates their auto-update

briefdescription.para: stops updating validVerts(), validFaces(), numValidVerts(), numValidFaces() for parallel processing of mesh parts
returns whether the methods validVerts(), validFaces(), numValidVerts(), numValidFaces() can be called

detaileddescription.para: for incident vertices and faces of given edges, remember one of them as edgeWithOrg and edgeWithLeft; this is important in parallel algorithms where other edges may change but stable ones will survive

briefdescription.para: constructs triangular grid mesh topology in parallel

detaileddescription.para: verifies that all internal data structures are valid; if allVerts=true then checks in addition that all not-lone edges have valid vertices on both ends
computes from edges_ all remaining fields: 1) numValidVerts_, 2) validVerts_, 3) edgePerVertex_, 4) numValidFaces_, 5) validFaces_, 6) edgePerFace_

briefdescription.para: sets new origin to the full origin ring including this edge, without updating edgePerVertex_ table
writes all valid edge_.org by calling setOrg_ for each valid edgePerVertex_
sets new left face to the full left ring including this edge, without updating edgePerFace_ table
writes all valid edge_.left by calling setLeft_ for each valid edgePerFace_

memberdef.argsstring: (HalfEdgeRecord &r, const FM &fmap, const VM &vmap, const WEM &emap) const
memberdef.name: translateNoFlip_

briefdescription.para: translates all fields in the record for this edge given maps

memberdef.argsstring: (HalfEdgeRecord &r, HalfEdgeRecord &rsym, const FM &fmap, const VM &vmap, const WEM &emap, bool flipOrientation) const
memberdef.name: translate_

detaileddescription.para: Mesh Topology
